stem: A agricultural research study surveys 30 seeds planted and observes 10 positive outcomes out of 30 (33.3%). Estimate the true germination rate and provide a 95% confidence interval.

ground_truth.answer: Sample proportion: 0.3333. Wilson 95% CI: [0.1923, 0.5122]. Point estimate: 0.3522.

common_failure_modes: Using the Wald interval (p̂ ± z√(p̂(1-p̂)/n)) which performs poorly for small samples
Not reporting the confidence interval alongside the point estimate
Not recognizing that with n=30, the normal approximation may be poor
